topic: Hubble constant — verdict reclassification (Misleading -> Self-Contradicted) per EXP-295
change_summary: Analyst (EXP-295) recommends Misleading -> Self-Contradicted under sub_rationale 'category-error import (MOND grouping)'. This curmudgeon review verifies the reclassification specifically for WIN-047.
our_argument_summary: WIN-047 currently sits at verdict 'Misleading' with finding 'Dome claims low-z deviations support aetheric redshift, but provides no photon-aether interaction equation or redshift mechanism'. The detail_evidence makes a stronger argument than the verdict label carries: it explicitly states that the dome's firmament extends to ~9,086 km, yet galaxies are observed at millions of light-years, and the dome model provides no spatial framework for extragalactic objects -- without one, any claim about their redshift behavior is physically undefined. That language meets the existing Self-Contradicted pattern (2) -- structural-coherence failures where the dome's own commitments preclude the WIN claim on the dome's own terms -- already used for WIN-053 (ring-magnet flux conservation) and WIN-067 (Antarctic gravity rim).

endorsement.rationale: The SC reading is justified by the existing detail_evidence prose. The analyst's quoted text (no spatial framework for extragalactic objects ... physically undefined) is verbatim in the current WIN-047 detail_evidence (data/wins.json:WIN-047:detail_evidence). The dome's firmament_height=9086km parameter is explicit and propagates throughout the model (WIN-029 already SC; coordinates.html parameter table). The WIN cites Hubble's Law, which is measured on extragalactic distances -- Pantheon+ supernovae from z=0.001 to z=2.26, distances of order 10^25 m. The dome's bounded firmament (~10^7 m) and the WIN's confirming evidence (10^25 m) cannot both be held; that is the SC pattern. The verdict label is being aligned with what the prose already argues -- not introducing a new framework reading.
endorsement.pattern_match: WIN-053 and WIN-067 are the existing SC precedents for pattern (2) -- dome's commitments preclude WIN claim on dome's own terms. WIN-047 fits that pattern by the same reasoning: bounded-firmament parameter (commitment) precludes galactic-scale redshift measurements (WIN claim) from serving as confirmation.

relevant_files: raw-text/monitor.py (not re-examined this cycle -- last validated 2026-04-08 cycle 2)
dome_code_analysis.note: WIN-047 code_analysis tags were validated in c2; this cycle is a verdict-label change, not a code-tag change. Tags below carry forward from c2 unchanged.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: Carried forward from c2 -- no monitor.py domain tracks H_0 specifically; the dome adopts the standard value without runtime check.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: Carried forward from c2 -- H_0 = 67-74 km/s/Mpc is textbook; the dome adopts it and rebrands the redshift mechanism without producing a different numerical prediction.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: Carried forward from c2 -- Hubble's Law has been measured since 1929; the dome's monitor script imported the modern value as the predicted value, with the prediction and confirming data committed simultaneously per the dome's own timestamp policy.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: Carried forward from c2 -- no derivation chain from disc_radius/firmament_height/sun_altitude/moon_altitude to H_0; the value is adopted, not produced. The dome's invocation of lambda_A = 4283 Mpc as 'aetheric wavelength' is itself c/H_0 = 4282.7 Mpc with no independent derivation (see WIN-054 detail_evidence).

holes_found.description: EXP-295 expansion (counter_argument_a_addressed.rationale) characterizes WIN-054's existing detail_evidence as already presenting an inescapable dilemma structure (either galaxies exist at the claimed distances, falsifying dome geometry; or they are illusions, in which case their measured properties cannot serve as evidence). On direct read, WIN-054's detail_evidence does NOT structure the contradiction as a dual-horn dilemma -- it argues 'there is no spatial framework in which galaxy clusters at cosmological distances can exist' and 'that is not accommodation -- it is silence'. The contradiction is one-sided in the prose: bounded firmament forecloses the cited evidence. The either/or framing is the analyst's gloss in the EXP narrative and the proposed Part 6.13 subsection, not a verbatim claim about WIN-054's existing prose. This does not undermine the SC reclassification of WIN-047 specifically -- WIN-047's own prose ('no spatial framework for extragalactic objects ... physically undefined') is sound on its own -- but it slightly weakens the EXP's appeal-to-existing-prose argument when applied to WIN-054 across the package.
holes_found.recommendation: When the decider integrates EXP-295, soften the 'WIN-054 explicitly presents the inescapable dilemma' phrasing in any operator-facing or audit-trail text. The WIN-054 prose argues structural incompatibility (bounded firmament vs galactic distances); the dilemma framing is a reasonable downstream gloss but not what the existing prose says verbatim. For WIN-047 specifically, this is not a blocker -- the SC pattern (2) reading is supported on its own terms by the bounded-firmament-vs-extragalactic-Hubble-Law argument.

holes_found.description: The 'category-error import (MOND grouping)' sub_rationale label is somewhat stretched as applied to WIN-047. WIN-047 is a Hubble-Law / cosmic-redshift WIN; it predates and is independent of the MOND-curated anomaly list (Hubble's Law is measured by all cosmologists, not specifically marshaled by MOND advocates). The other four package WINs (048 Axis of Evil, 052 RAR, 054 El Gordo, 055 Cepheid ladder) are tighter fits to the MOND-anomaly-list provenance argument. WIN-047 fits the SC reclassification primarily on the bounded-firmament-vs-galactic-distances argument, and only secondarily on the import-burst grouping. The analyst's commit-history argument (eleven WINs in 32 minutes including WIN-047) makes the import claim defensible, but the strength of the SC reclassification for WIN-047 rests on the structural argument, not on the MOND-grouping provenance.
holes_found.recommendation: When the EXP-295-P1-WIN-047-verdict patch lands, the sub_rationale can stay as drafted (it does point to Part 6.13 for the cross-cutting argument) -- but the WIN-047 finding should foreground the structural argument ('Framework-level contradiction (bounded firmament cannot accommodate galaxies whose redshift the dome adopts as confirmation)') as the primary basis, with the MOND grouping as secondary context. The proposed append_prefix already does this. No patch change required; flagging for any future audit-trail prose.

holes_found.description: The proposed tldr_verdict for WIN-047 is 38 words, up from 24. The new version carries two layers (Misleading-style 'no derivation, no mechanism' AND SC-style 'bounded firmament cannot accommodate referents'). It scans cleanly in two sentences. Slight risk that the second sentence ('The two cannot both be held') is dependency-tight on the first; a non-expert reader unfamiliar with the prior context may read 'the two' as the standard cosmology vs aetheric attenuation pair (which are claims, not commitments) rather than the bounded-firmament parameter vs the galactic-scale Hubble Law referent (the actual contradiction).
holes_found.recommendation: Consider tightening the closing line to '-- and the dome cannot publish a maximum spatial extent of 30,000 km while citing measurements at galactic distances as confirmation.' This explicitly names what 'cannot both be held' refers to and removes the deictic ambiguity. Optional improvement; the current draft is acceptable.

holes_found.description: Curmudgeon spot-check confirms WIN-048's 'LambdaCDM Counter-Prediction' sub-label exists verbatim in detail_evidence ('The dome categorizes this as a LambdaCDM Counter-Prediction'). The routing note asked for this verification; confirmed. Not a hole on WIN-047 specifically -- flagged here because the same queue (queue_ids 318-322) shares routing context.
holes_found.recommendation: No action; verification confirms the EXP-295 claim about WIN-048's existing sub-label.

stronger_arguments.description: The SC reclassification of WIN-047 could be sharpened by making the dimensional-incompatibility argument quantitative in the new tldr_verdict or finding. The bounded firmament is ~10^7 m. The Hubble flow is measured at z=0.001 (~4 Mpc, ~10^23 m) and extends to z=2.26 (~10^26 m). The ratio is 16 orders of magnitude. Naming the ratio in the WIN-047 finding (or in Part 6.13) makes the framework-level contradiction visceral in a way 'cannot accommodate' alone does not.

An explicit cross-link from WIN-047's detail prose to WIN-029 (firmament_height parameter, already SC) would tighten the structural argument. Currently WIN-047 says 'the dome's firmament extends to ~9,086 km' as a bare assertion of fact. Linking to WIN-029 -- which establishes that this parameter is the dome's own published commitment -- converts the assertion to an audited claim. This is the same move WIN-048's existing 'Self-contradiction with WIN-034' inline does for the copper-firmament case.
sources: data/wins.json:WIN-048 (existing inline self-contradiction reference for analogous structure)

kernel_of_truth.description: The dome author has correctly identified that Hubble's Law is one of the most precisely confirmed cosmological relationships and that low-z deviations have generated genuine tension in standard cosmology (the Hubble tension between SH0ES at 73.04 km/s/Mpc and Planck CMB at 67.4 km/s/Mpc, >5sigma). It is true that this tension is unresolved in standard physics (data/wins.json:WIN-047:detail_evidence). Importing it into the dome's WIN registry as evidence is a not-unreasonable instinct -- anomalies in the standard model are exactly the territory where alternative models would be expected to compete.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: The instinct to import standard-cosmology anomalies fails when the importing model has no spatial framework at the scales where the anomalies are measured. The dome's bounded firmament at ~9,086 km (data/wins.json:WIN-029, dome coordinates.html) cannot host the galactic and cosmological referents on which Hubble's Law is measured (data/wins.json:WIN-047:detail_evidence: Pantheon+ z=0.001 to z=2.26, gamma-ray bursts to z~8). The dome adopts H_0 = 67-74 km/s/Mpc -- the standard range -- without producing a different numerical prediction (data/wins.json:WIN-047:detail_evidence). Adopting the standard value while denying the spatial framework in which it is measured is the structural-coherence failure that defines Self-Contradicted pattern (2). The kernel-of-truth (the Hubble tension is real) does not transfer because the dome offers no derivation pathway from its own parameters to either competing value, and its own bounded-firmament parameter forecloses the derivation route.

package_summary_for_decider: ENDORSE the EXP-295 reclassification of WIN-047 from Misleading to Self-Contradicted. The proposed verdict change, finding append, tldr_verdict rewrite, and sub_rationale addition are all sound. Two moderate findings (one about cross-package phrasing on WIN-054, one about MOND-grouping label fit for WIN-047 specifically) are clarifications not blockers -- neither requires a patch change. Two minor findings flag potential tightenings (tldr deixis, WIN-029 cross-link). The decider may proceed with the EXP-295-P1 patch as drafted; the curmudgeon's per-WIN reviews for queue_ids 319-322 will follow on subsequent runs (singleton-only this run because the gate criterion 'no verdict change recommended' is violated by definition for verdict-review items).
deeper_analysis: The SC reclassification of WIN-047 sits at the intersection of two reading frames. Frame A: Misleading is the right verdict because the dome adopts standard values without derivation and provides no falsifiable mechanism -- i.e., the WIN is empirically inert. Frame B: SC is the right verdict because the dome's own bounded-firmament commitment forecloses the spatial framework in which the WIN's confirming evidence is measured -- i.e., the WIN is structurally inert. Both frames apply. The analyst's argument is that Frame B is stronger and is what the existing detail_evidence prose already argues; the prose says 'physically undefined' which is Frame B language, not 'no mechanism therefore unfalsifiable' which would be Frame A language. The verdict label has trailed the prose. The reclassification brings the label up to match. From a defender's perspective, the strongest pushback is 'the dome is silent on extragalactic ontology, not denying it' -- but the dome's published unified-theory parameter table provides no broader cosmology, and silence-plus-bounded-firmament is positive contradiction when the WIN cites galactic-scale measurements as confirmation. EXP-295's anticipated_objections (objection c) addresses this exactly and the rebuttal is sound. Net: ENDORSE.
